title: Confirmation of Motion
description: Filed to confirm that your motion is going ahead and that you will attend the hearing. Must be filed by 2:00 PM at least 3 business days before your motion date. If you miss this deadline, your motion may be cancelled. You must also give a copy to the other party before filing with the court.

steps.title: Court & File Information
steps.description: Enter the court location and file number.

fields.label: Case Management Judge (if any)
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Honourable Justice A. Lee
fields.hint: If a case management judge has been assigned to your case, enter their name here. Leave blank if none.

steps.title: Who Is Filing This Confirmation
steps.description: Identify yourself and whether you have spoken with the other party.

fields.label: My name is (full legal name) and I am:
Your Full Legal Name
fields.hint: Enter the name of the person or lawyer filing this form.

fields.label: Have you conferred with the opposing counsel or party about the issues, motion material, and time estimates?
fields.hint: The Family Law Rules require the parties or their counsel to confer, or attempt to confer, orally or in writing before filing this confirmation.

fields.label: Reason for not conferring with the other party:
fields.placeholder: e.g. I attempted to reach the other party by phone and email on [date] but received no response. / A restraining order prohibits direct communication.
fields.hint: Provide a specific reason. If there is a court order prohibiting communication, state that here.

steps.title: Motion Status
steps.description: Tell the court what is happening with your motion.

fields.label: Scheduled Date of Motion
fields.hint: The date the motion is scheduled to be heard, as shown on your Notice of Motion (Form 14).

fields.label: Scheduled Time of Motion
fields.hint: The time the motion is scheduled, as shown on your Notice of Motion (Form 14).

fields.label: Has a case conference been held in this case?

options.label: Yes — a case conference was held
No — but an exception applies (describe below)
Not required for this type of motion

fields.hint: Generally, a case conference must be held before a motion can be heard, unless the motion is urgent or the court grants an exception.

fields.label: Describe why no case conference was held:
fields.placeholder: e.g. This is an urgent motion for a temporary restraining order. / The court granted leave to bring this motion without a prior conference by order dated [date].

options.label: Going ahead on the issues listed below
Going ahead for a consent order
Being adjourned on consent to a future date
Going ahead for a contested adjournment asked for by one party

fields.label: Adjourned for what type of event?
fields.placeholder: e.g. settlement conference, trial management conference

fields.label: Name of party asking for the contested adjournment:
Reason for the contested adjournment request:
fields.placeholder: e.g. The applicant is requesting an adjournment due to a medical appointment on the scheduled date.

fields.label: Describe what is happening:
fields.placeholder: Describe the current status of the motion.

steps.title: Issues, Documents & Time Estimate
steps.description: List the issues, documents for the judge, and how much time you need.

fields.label: Issues going ahead at this motion (list each issue):
fields.placeholder:
e.g.
a) Temporary decision-making responsibility for the child Emma Smith
b) Temporary parenting time schedule
c) Temporary child support in the amount of $X per month
fields.hint: List every issue the judge will be deciding at this motion. Be specific — include the type of order and the child's name where applicable.

fields.label: Documents the judge should read (list each document, volume, and tab number in the Continuing Record):
fields.placeholder:
e.g.
a) Notice of Motion (Form 14) — Volume 1, Tab A
b) Affidavit of Jane Smith sworn June 15, 2025 (Form 14A) — Volume 1, Tab B
c) Financial Statement of Jane Smith (Form 13) — Volume 2, Tab C
d) Affidavit of Service (Form 6B) — Volume 1, Tab D
fields.hint: Organize by volume and tab as they appear in the Continuing Record. The judge uses this list to locate your materials quickly.

fields.label: I confirm that I will bring a complete copy of the Continuing Record to the motion.
The presiding judge will be (if known):
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Honourable Justice A. Lee
fields.hint: Leave blank if you do not know who will hear the motion.

fields.label: Time estimate — Applicant (minutes):
fields.hint: Estimate how many minutes the applicant (or their lawyer) will need to present arguments.

fields.label: Time estimate — Respondent (minutes):
fields.hint: Estimate how many minutes the respondent (or their lawyer) will need.

fields.label: Time estimate — Reply (minutes, if any):
fields.hint: Estimate any additional time needed for reply submissions. Often 0 or 5 minutes.

steps.title: Delivery & Deadline Reminder
steps.description: Confirm you have given a copy to the other party and understand the filing deadline.

fields.label: Filing Deadline — Important
fields.content:
You must file this Confirmation of Motion with the court no later than 2:00 PM, at least 3 business days before your motion date.

If you miss this deadline, your motion may be cancelled and you will have to get a new date.

You can file this form:
• In person at the courthouse
• By email (if your court allows it) through Justice Services Online

Note: Courts no longer accept this form by fax.

fields.label: I confirm that I have given (or will give) a copy of this Confirmation to the other party or their lawyer before filing it with the court.
fields.hint: The Family Law Rules require you to deliver a copy to the other side before filing. You do NOT need to file a Form 6B (Affidavit of Service) for this particular form.

fields.label: Date of Signature
fields.hint: Enter the date you are signing this confirmation.

fields.label: I confirm that all information in this Confirmation of Motion is accurate.
Hearth & Page Notice
fields.content: This form was prepared using Hearth & Page (hearthandpage.ca). File it with the court no later than 2:00 PM, 3 business days before your motion date. Give the other party a copy first. Hearth & Page is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice.
